```python
import smtplib
from email.message import EmailMessage
from typing import List, Dict

class EmailSender:
    def __init__(self, smtp_server: str, smtp_port: int, username: str, password: str, use_tls: bool = True):
        """
        Initialize the email sender with SMTP server details.
        """
        self.smtp_server = smtp_server
        self.smtp_port = smtp_port
        self.username = username
        self.password = password
        self.use_tls = use_tls

    def send_email(self, recipient: str, subject: str, body: str) -> bool:
        """
        Sends a single email. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.
        """
        try:
            msg = EmailMessage()
            msg["From"] = self.username
            msg["To"] = recipient
            msg["Subject"] = subject
            msg.set_content(body)

            with smtplib.SMTP(self.smtp_server, self.smtp_port) as server:
                if self.use_tls:
                    server.starttls()
                server.login(self.username, self.password)
                server.send_message(msg)
            print(f"Email sent to {recipient} with subject '{subject}'")
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Failed to send email to {recipient}: {e}")
            return False

    def send_notification(self, recipients: List[str], notification_subject: str, notification_body: str) -> Dict[str, bool]:
        """
        Send a generic notification email to multiple recipients.
        Returns a dict mapping each recipient to True/False based on success.
        """
        results = {}
        for email in recipients:
            success = self.send_email(email, notification_subject, notification_body)
            results[email] = success
        return results

    def send_interview_invite(self, candidate_email: str, candidate_name: str, interview_date: str, instructions: str) -> bool:
        """
        Send an interview invitation to a single candidate.
        """
        subject = f"Interview Invitation for {candidate_name}"
        body = (
            f"Hello {candidate_name},\n\n"
            f"You are invited to an interview on {interview_date}.\n"
            f"Instructions:\n{instructions}\n\n"
            "Please reply to confirm your availability.\n\n"
            "Best regards,\n"
            "Recruitment Team"
        )
        return self.send_email(candidate_email, subject, body)

# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    smtp_config = {
        "smtp_server": "smtp.example.com",
        "smtp_port": 587,
        "username": "recruiter@example.com",
        "password": "securepassword",
        "use_tls": True
    }

    email_sender = EmailSender(**smtp_config)

    # Send a generic notification
    notifications = email_sender.send_notification(
        recipients=["applicant1@example.com", "applicant2@example.com"],
        notification_subject="Application Received",
        notification_body="Thank you for applying. We have received your application and will be in touch."
    )
    print("Notification results:", notifications)

    # Send an interview invite
    invite_success = email_sender.send_interview_invite(
        candidate_email="candidate@example.com",
        candidate_name="Jane Doe",
        interview_date="2024-07-01 10:00 AM",
        instructions="Please join the Zoom meeting with the link provided in the calendar invite."
    )
    print("Interview invite sent:", invite_success)
```
